input.prompt:
My MSTest v3 project uses [ExpectedException] on several test methods.
After upgrading to MSTest v4, these no longer compile. What's the replacement?

data.content:
My MSTest v3 project uses [ExpectedException] on several test methods.
After upgrading to MSTest v4, these no longer compile. What's the replacement?
data.transformedContent:
<current_datetime>2026-05-08T03:00:53.876Z</current_datetime>

My MSTest v3 project uses [ExpectedException] on several test methods.
After upgrading to MSTest v4, these no longer compile. What's the replacement?

input.initialPrompt:
My MSTest v3 project uses [ExpectedException] on several test methods.
After upgrading to MSTest v4, these no longer compile. What's the replacement?

data.reasoningText: The user is asking about migrating from MSTest v3 to v4, specifically about the `[ExpectedException]` attribute which has been removed. Let me look at their test file to understand what they have and provide the correct replacement.

data.content:
MSTest v4 removed `[ExpectedException]`. The replacement is `Assert.ThrowsExactly<T>()` (matches exact type) or `Assert.ThrowsException<T>()` (matches derived types too).

Let me update your project:
data.reasoningText:
MSTest v4 removed `[ExpectedException]`. The replacement is `Assert.ThrowsExactly<T>()` (or `Assert.ThrowsException<T>()` which has been available since earlier versions). In MSTest v4, `Assert.ThrowsExactly<T>` is the preferred approach.

Let me update the test file to replace `[ExpectedException]` with `Assert.ThrowsExactly<T>()`, and also update the package version to v4.

toolCalls.name: edit
